findings.description: mask_middle with visible=0 hits value[-0:] which is value[0:], so the masker appends the ENTIRE plaintext after the mask (e.g. 'secret' -> '******secret') — a redaction routine that helpfully un-redacts the secret it was hired to hide.
Same visible=0 path returns a string of length 2*len(value) (full mask + full original), silently breaking the implied 'output length == input length' invariant of every other branch.

slugify does slug[:max_length].rstrip('-') AFTER the inner strip('-'), so truncation can re-expose a trailing hyphen the earlier cleanup removed, yielding a slug that ends in '-' anyway when the cut lands on one.
truncate returns suffix[:limit] when len(suffix) >= limit, emitting a meaningless fragment like '.' or '…' with zero original content and no caller signal that the text was fully discarded.

truncate counts characters while the default suffix '…' is a multi-byte codepoint; any byte-budgeted caller (HTTP headers, fixed-width columns) silently overruns despite the docstring's 'never exceeds it' promise.
indent_block skips indentation for whitespace-only lines because line.strip() is falsy, so a '   ' line stays un-indented despite being non-empty per the docstring's wording.
collapse_whitespace's \s+ swallows newlines/tabs, silently flattening multi-line input to a single line — fine if intended, a surprise if the caller expected per-line collapsing.
pluralize advertises a 'naive English plural' yet only ever appends 's' (box -> boxs, baby -> babys); the docstring's hedge does not save callers who trusted the word 'plural'.
